title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: Eating Well, Eating Slowly: The Slow Food Philosophy
content.text: In 1989, a group of Italian food activists gathered in Paris and signed a founding manifesto that began with an unusual declaration: that pleasure was a right worth defending. Their organisation, Slow Food, had been born in direct opposition to the opening of a McDonald's restaurant near the Spanish Steps in Rome two years earlier. (37) [37] El movimiento surgió como reacción a la comida rápida. [/37] What started as a local Italian protest has since grown into a global movement with chapters in over one hundred and sixty countries. || The Slow Food manifesto argued that industrial food production was degrading not only the quality of what people ate but also the cultural traditions and environmental resources tied to food. (38) [38] El manifiesto denunciaba el impacto cultural y ambiental de la alimentación industrial. [/38] Taste itself, the founders argued, was being standardised and impoverished, and the art of cooking — with its regional variations, seasonal rhythms and inherited wisdom — was under serious threat. || One of Slow Food's most significant initiatives is the Ark of Taste, a catalogue of foods at risk of disappearing. (39) [39] El Arca del Gusto documenta alimentos tradicionales en peligro de extinción. [/39] It now contains over five thousand entries: ancient grain varieties, indigenous vegetable breeds, traditional cheese-making methods, artisanal cured meats and endangered fish species. By naming and celebrating these products, Slow Food hopes to create economic conditions in which small-scale producers can survive. || The movement also founded Terra Madre, a biennial gathering held in Turin. (40) [40] Terra Madre reúne a productores de todo el mundo. [/40] Small-scale farmers, fishermen, pastoralists and food artisans travel from every continent to share knowledge, discuss challenges and trade products. It is, in effect, a parliament of food producers, advocating for a more just and sustainable food system. || Critics have accused Slow Food of being an elitist movement — one that celebrates expensive artisan products accessible only to affluent consumers. (41) [41] La crítica de elitismo ha sido un desafío persistente para el movimiento. [/41] The organisation has responded by expanding its work in schools, urban gardens and low-income communities, and by advocating for food policies that make good food accessible to all, not just those who can afford a premium. || Carlo Petrini, the movement's founder, often says that a good, clean and fair food system is not a luxury but a necessity. (42) [42] Para Petrini, la alimentación justa es una cuestión de derechos, no de privilegio. [/42] The task ahead, he acknowledges, is enormous — but the meal on the table, he insists, is always a good place to start.

paragraphs.A: Slow Food has acknowledged these concerns and has worked actively to broaden its social and economic reach.
paragraphs.B: Each entry represents not just a food product but a living piece of cultural heritage and biodiversity.
paragraphs.C: The event attracts thousands of delegates and is accompanied by a vast open food market that draws visitors from around the world.
paragraphs.D: It also challenged the loss of biodiversity that accompanied the narrowing of global food choices to a small number of standardised varieties.
paragraphs.E: Petrini argues that what people eat is inseparable from how they live, who they are and what kind of world they want.
paragraphs.F: The restaurant became a symbol of everything the movement's founders believed was wrong with modern food culture.
paragraphs.G: That founding philosophy has remained essentially unchanged, even as the organisation has grown far beyond its Italian roots.

questions.explanation: F conecta el McDonald's de Roma con el simbolismo que motivó la protesta antes de describir el crecimiento global del movimiento.
D añade la pérdida de biodiversidad como segunda crítica del manifiesto, completando los argumentos medioambientales y culturales.
B explica el significado de cada entrada del Arca del Gusto como patrimonio cultural y de biodiversidad antes de dar cifras.
C describe la escala del evento Terra Madre y el mercado de alimentos que lo acompaña, antes de explicar su función política.
A introduce la respuesta de Slow Food a las críticas de elitismo antes de los detalles sobre escuelas y comunidades.
E expresa la filosofía integral de Petrini sobre comida e identidad, anticipando su mensaje final sobre el cambio sistémico.
